title: Symbol Shelter — Konpo Studio
description: Rejected logo marks by Konpo Studio, catalogued and up for adoption.

items.title: SYM-096 — Lantern
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — Nested meridians swelling to a sphere, drawn twice and mirrored. Every rib shares the poles. A wireframe globe that reads at any size.

items.title: SYM-095 — Turbine
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — A sliced orb with fins stepping off both hemispheres. Spin implied, warranty void. The steps descend in even increments toward the poles.

items.title: SYM-094 — Marigold
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — Eight lobes and a core cut from one piece. The stamp version of a garden. Ink it and press — it holds at any size.

items.title: SYM-093 — Anemone
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — Eight petals drawn in one heavy contour. The flower with outdoor durability. The stroke never varies; the corners carry the character.

items.title: SYM-090 — Warp
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Two strokes woven into peaks, over-under-over. The loom’s first opinion. Line weight stays constant through every crossing.

items.title: SYM-089 — Cradle
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — A dome coming to rest in its rim. Touchdown held one frame before contact. The gap between mass and cradle is the entire drama.

items.title: SYM-086 — Latitude
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Horizontal ellipses stacked into a globe. Meridian’s level-headed sibling. The rings compress toward the poles at true projection.

items.title: SYM-085 — Tally
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — Counters in framed cells — the score kept honest. The frame divides before the dots decide. A game where the grid always wins.

items.title: SYM-084 — Waveform
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Vertical strokes swelling to an ellipse. Sound pressure, set in type. The envelope is symmetric; the noise is disciplined.

items.title: SYM-083 — Aperture
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — A ring, a lid, a pupil. The lens mid-blink. The bar sits exactly on the horizontal axis — the calm before the shutter.

items.title: SYM-082 — Ripple
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Rings inside rings, weighted low in the frame. A drop landed and the mark kept the evidence. Amplitude decays outward, as physics asks.

items.title: SYM-081 — Lune
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Crescent slivers nested inside an O. Geometry’s name for a moon-shape; the client said banana. The moons win on paper.

items.title: SYM-080 — Windrose
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Spokes tipped with direction markers, set at true intervals. A compass that lost its map. North is wherever you hang it.

items.title: SYM-079 — Daisy
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — Six outline petals around an open eye. The lawn-variety flower, drafted properly. Petal roots share one circle; nothing droops.

items.title: SYM-078 — Helm
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — A dome strapped with a low band and side curls. Part helmet, part kettle — the debate was never settled. The strap holds either story on.

items.title: SYM-077 — Louver
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Horizontal slats filling a softened square. Ventilation for a brand. The slat gap equals the slat height, which is why it hums.

items.title: SYM-075 — Dune
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — A solid dome settling past its baseline. Weather did the styling. The flat is not a crop — it is where the mass came to rest.

items.title: SYM-074 — Knot
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — One continuous line looping through four lobes. No start, no end, no slack. Trace it with a finger; you arrive where you began.

items.title: SYM-073 — Quartet
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Four dashed rings, tangent in a 2×2. Ensemble playing; nobody solos. The dashes sync at every meeting point — that took an afternoon.

items.title: SYM-072 — Coil
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Nested arcs wound into a sphere with a twist at the equator. A spring at rest, drawn under tension. The line spacing is the energy diagram.

items.title: SYM-069 — Phases
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Crescents overlapping left to right — a moon month compressed into one lockup. Each phase eclipses the last. Time reads as thickness.

items.title: SYM-068 — Cloister
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Four corner arcs enclosing a courtyard of air. The frame is the building. Close the gaps and it dies; the openings are the architecture.

items.title: SYM-067 — Barcode
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Vertical bars packed into an ellipse. Scans as an orb; scans as nothing else. The bar rhythm stays even — the silhouette does the bending.

items.title: SYM-062 — Stitch
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — A cross assembled from square counters on a strict grid. Embroidery logic, machine confidence. Zoom in and it never gets smoother.

items.title: SYM-061 — Union
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Two open rings joined at the seam. The merger that actually closed. Their gaps face away from each other — nobody’s exiting.

items.title: SYM-059 — Whorl
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Concentric ellipses packed until they vibrate. Stare responsibly. The spacing is optically corrected, which only makes it worse.

items.title: SYM-058 — Venn
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Two circles, one intersection, no labels. Every strategy deck, distilled. The overlap is set at exactly one third — someone measured.

items.title: SYM-056 — Orbit
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Three ellipses sharing a nucleus, drawn with a loose wrist. The atom before the standards manual arrived. Charmingly out of tolerance.

items.title: SYM-055 — Visor
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — An orb with two smile-lines low on the face. Reads as a helmet from across the room, a kettle up close. Nobody would arbitrate.

items.title: SYM-053 — Tether
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Two C-forms locked wrist to wrist. The chain link that wanted a serif. Pull either end and the counters only close tighter.

items.title: SYM-052 — Meridian
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — A sphere sliced by longitudes, solid where it matters. Globe logic without the map. The slices thin toward the poles, as they should.

items.title: SYM-050 — Dahlia
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — Eight solid petals around a core dot, press-fit with no overlaps. The flower reduced to stamping-die parts. Would survive a keychain.

items.title: SYM-049 — Argyle
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — A lens filled with diagonal checkers — forty-five degrees of knitwear geometry. The counters shrink toward the tips. Grandfather-approved.

items.title: SYM-048 — Garland
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Six small rings strung around a seventh. A molecule dressed for a party. Every bond is a shared tangent, checked twice.

items.title: SYM-046 — Synapse
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Four pill-forms firing toward a center that isn’t drawn. The gap carries the charge. Symmetry keeps the voltage even.

items.title: SYM-045 — Duplex
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Half square, half circle, one silhouette. The two tenants never met; the party wall is dead straight. A duplex with excellent zoning.

items.title: SYM-044 — Gyre
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Two comma-forms chasing each other around a still center. Motion with the brakes on. The eye completes a rotation the geometry only implies.

items.title: SYM-043 — Tangent
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — A square and a circle meeting at exactly one point of agreement. Neither yields. The overlap is small enough to be a decision, not an accident.

items.title: SYM-042 — Medallion
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — Rings, petals and a rounded frame in strict concentricity. The ceremonial one — built for embossing. Every band shares the same center point.

items.title: SYM-041 — Parquet
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Dot-and-tile pairs in a 2×2 rotation. Flooring logic at logo scale. Rotate any unit 90° and the pattern heals itself.

items.title: SYM-040 — Circuit
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Paired dots and pills stepping up the diagonal. A circuit board that learned composition. Current flows corner to corner, or it would if it could.

items.title: SYM-039 — Relay
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Two pointed nodes flanking a dot — a signal caught mid-pass. The taper sets the direction of travel. Read left to right, like everything else here.

items.title: SYM-038 — Clover
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — A disc split into four kissing petals. The negative cross does the work; the petals just hold their corners. Luck, engineered.

items.title: SYM-035 — Molecule
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Dots and bonds laid on one axis — a compound diagrammed in plan view. Every interval is a multiple of the dot radius. Chemistry, house style.

items.title: SYM-030 — Socket
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — A disc with its core exposed and one channel out. Something used to plug in here. The absence is drawn more carefully than the presence.

items.title: SYM-029 — Rosette
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Four rings pinched around a square heart. The dashes were load-bearing — remove one and the square dissolves. Ornament with a skeleton.

items.title: SYM-028 — Hexad
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Six wedges locked into a hexagon, meeting at a pinpoint center. Cut, don’t draw. Rotational symmetry does the talking; the gaps do the counting.

items.title: SYM-027 — Casement
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Four rounded panes split by a hairline mullion. A window with nothing behind it yet. The radii agree at every corner, which is the whole job.

items.title: SYM-026 — Bloom
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — A ring of eight dots crowding a clustered core. Spacing tightens toward the middle like a real bouquet. The client called it too happy; it is.

items.title: SYM-024 — Corolla
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — Five petal-rings around an open center, pitched at 72°. Each ring borrows its neighbor’s edge. Botany redrawn by compass and patience.

items.title: SYM-023 — Enso
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — One stroke, almost closed, weight easing at the release. The gap is the point. The most hand-drawn thing in a catalogue of compasses.

items.title: SYM-022 — Swarm
items.content_text: Abstract · ADOPTED · 06.2026 — Seven equal dots in loose hexagonal formation. Ordered enough to feel deliberate, loose enough to feel alive. Count them twice; the count holds.

items.title: SYM-021 — Domino
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — Two upright tiles, four counters, one connecting bar. Game pieces posing as a system diagram. The pips align to the grid, not to luck.

items.title: SYM-020 — Binary
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED — Two discs sharing one edge, drawn as a single mass. One reads full, the other clipped — a pair before parts. The seam is the signature.

items.title: SYM-019 — Asterisk
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — Six serifed spokes off a common hub, tips flared like type. The footnote that wanted to be the headline. Set at even intervals, no favorites.

items.title: SYM-018 — Iris
items.content_text: Pictorial · REJECTED — A ring holding five vertical lenses, widest at the axis. Read it as an eye or an onion cross-section; both camps were loud. The ring keeps the peace.

items.title: SYM-017 — Facet
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED — A ring milled into eight flats — no curves survive. The two halves stop just short of touching at the waist. Gem-cutter logic applied to an O.

items.title: SYM-016 — Duet
items.content_text: Monogram · REJECTED — Two open rings, each broken exactly where the other passes through. Same radius, same weight, opposite entries. A handshake drawn in negative space.

items.title: SYM-015 — Tessera
items.content_text: Pictorial · ADOPTED · 05.2026 — A circle-packing tile. One full circle at center, part-circles on every edge of the 90u frame, all sharing the same R 21.4. Repeat it and it tiles clean. The unit is the pattern.

items.title: SYM-014 — Trinode
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED · TOO ABSTRACT — Three R 13.9 nodes joined by one continuous bond. The top pair sits 54.2u apart and the apex closes at exactly 90 degrees. A molecule that behaves like a monogram.

items.title: SYM-013 — Quatre
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED · TOO EDGY — Four R 15 circles at 90 degrees, each with its own keyhole. Strict compass layout, loose personality.

items.title: SYM-012 — Pentad
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED · TOO DECORATIVE — Five arms on a 72 degree pitch around a 25.6u orbit. Pentagonal symmetry, no pentagon in sight.

items.title: SYM-011 — G Mark
items.content_text: Monogram · REJECTED · TOO LITERAL — One R 36 circle carries everything. The counter is its own 13.4 circle snapped inside the curve, and the terminal cuts flat against the frame edge.

items.title: SYM-010 — Link
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED · TOO FRIENDLY — Two hooks, same R 19, rotated 180 degrees around the center point. Point symmetry doing the work of a whole mark.

items.title: SYM-007 — Rotor
items.content_text: Abstract · REJECTED · TOO BUSY — Two rings and two squares rotated into a single rotor. The arcs resolve against R 40 and R 28.9 from one shared center, and the squares carry a deliberate 1 degree tilt at 180 degrees to each other. Nothing here exists outside those two radii.

items.title: SYM-006 — Dial
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED · TOO FLOWERY — Four R 15 circles locked to the axes on a 30u pitch, each one hatched differently. An instrument panel disguised as a mark.

items.title: SYM-005 — Ribbon C
items.content_text: Monogram · REJECTED · TOO CLEVER — One folded plane inside a 72 by 80 frame. The crease runs at 53 degrees and carries the whole gesture.

items.title: SYM-004 — Bracket
items.content_text: Monogram · REJECTED · TOO SQUARE — Two 24u blocks and a hooked spine with a 12u radius. A letterform doing structural work.

items.title: SYM-002 — Junction
items.content_text: Geometric · REJECTED · TOO SYMMETRICAL — Four quarter arcs, each anchored dead on a corner point. The bars split the middle at 6.7u. Reads like a road interchange because it's built like one.

items.title: SYM-001 — CO Monogram
items.content_text: Monogram · REJECTED · TOO CIRCULAR — Two letters, four radii. The C runs R 36 outside and R 24 inside, the O answers at 23 and 12, and the whole lockup shares one baseline. Nothing freehand about it.
